div1.entryFree: Sequester, vb. to separate: a poor --ed stag, As II, 1, 33. --ing from me all that time, acquaintance, custom and condition made tame and most familiar to my nature, Troil. III, 3, 8. why are you --ed from all your train, Tit. II, 3, 75 (séquestered? cf. the subst. in Oth. III, 4, 40).
